name: Great White Shark
summary: The great white shark, also known as the white shark, white pointer, or great white, is a large shark. It is closely related to the mako sharks, the porbeagle, and the salmon shark. It is a robustly built species with a grayish upperside and a white underside. The white shark is one of the largest living shark and fish species, being smaller than the whale shark and basking shark. It has about 300 triangular, serrated teeth that are continuously replaced. Its massive, fatty liver can reach over a quarter of its body weight, providing buoyancy and storing energy. The species is partially warm-blooded, an adaptation that allows it to remain active in colder waters.

wikipedia_sections.etymology: Etymology and naming The name 'great white shark' likely comes from the white underside exposed on beached sharks. The most common English names for the species include great white shark, white shark, and Australian variant white pointer. 6 7 These names refer to its white underside, which is noticeable in dead sharks lying upside down. 8 Colloquial use favours the name great white shark or simply great white, with great perhaps emphasizing the size and power of the species. Scientists typically use white shark, as no "lesser white shark" exists for comparison, 9 though some use white shark to

It is closely related to the mako sharks , the porbeagle , and the salmon shark . It is a robustly built species with a grayish upperside and a white underside. The white shark is one of the largest living shark and fish species, being smaller than the whale shark and basking shark . wikipedia_sections.habitat: White sharks inhabit tropical and temperate ocean waters around the world and can be found both along the coast and further out to sea. The authors conclude that "sharks don't make 'mistakes' but instead continually explore their environments and routinely investigate novel objects as potential prey by biting them". Western Australia implemented a shark cull program in 2013, but discontinued the following year in response to a recommendation by the Environmental Protection Authority . Environmental Biology of Fishes .
wikipedia_sections.hunting_diet: The white shark preys on marine mammals such as seals and dolphins , as well as fish, including other sharks, and cephalopods . It is also a prolific scavenger of whale carcasses. Though normally an apex predator , the species is sometimes preyed on by orcas . White sharks are generally solitary, but may gather in aggregations, particularly at feeding sites. Juvenile white sharks typically inhabit shallower water and are limited to feeding on smaller prey. The white shark has a fearsome reputation among the public.
wikipedia_sections.behavior: The species is partially warm-blooded , an adaptation that allows it to remain active in colder waters. 77 Behavior and ecology White shark surfacing at Dyer Island White sharks are estimated to swim 2.88–4.86 km/h (1.79–3.02 mph), 83 but can sprint up to 24.12 km/h (14.99 mph). 84 f One individual was recorded cruising at a sustained speed of 4.7 km/h (2.9 mph) while migrating, which is fast for a shark and more similar to fast-swimming tuna . 76 White sharks display various surface behaviors. 88 89 The white shark is generally considered to be a solitary species, though aggregations do occur
wikipedia_sections.reproduction: 64 The lipids and fatty acids provide the shark energy for travel and also power reproduction and growth. 62 63 One study concluded that a white shark liver is more energy-rich than whale blubber . 63 White sharks appear to have strong immune systems and can tolerate high amounts of toxic heavy metals like mercury and arsenic in their blood, more so than other vertebrates. 65 They are also documented to heal relatively quickly from even severe wounds, 66 and the species' genome shows "positive selection in key genes involved in the wound-healing process".
